API.description: An API (application programming interface) is a computing interface that defines interactions between multiple software or mixed hardware-software intermediaries. It defines the kinds of calls or requests that can be made, how to make them, the data formats that should be used, the conventions to follow, etc. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/API]

properties.name: createdBy
properties.description: The identity that created a characterization of a concept.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: externalReference
properties.description: Specifies a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: hasFacet
properties.description: Further sets of properties characterizing a concept based on the particular context of the class and of the particular instance of the concept being characterized.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: objectCreatedTime
properties.description: The time at which a characterization of a concept is created. This time pertains to the time of creating the record object, and is not an intrinsic characteristic of the concept.

properties.name: objectMarking
properties.description: Marking definitions to be applied to a particular concept characterization in its entirety.

properties.name: objectStatus
properties.description: The current state of formality and acceptance for a UCO object.

properties.name: specVersion
properties.description: The version of UCO ontology or subontology specification used to characterize a concept.

properties.name: tag
properties.description: A generic tag/label.

ARPCache.description: An ARP cache is a collection of Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) entries (mostly dynamic) that are created when an IP address is resolved to a MAC address (so the computer can effectively communicate with the IP address). [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ARP_cache]

AccountAuthenticationFacet.description: An account authentication facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the mechanism of accessing an account.

properties.name: password
properties.description: Specifies an authentication password.

properties.name: passwordLastChanged
properties.description: The date and time that the password was last changed.

properties.name: passwordType
properties.description: The type of password, for instance plain-text or encrypted.

AccountFacet.description: An account facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to an arrangement with an entity to enable and control the provision of some capability or service.

properties.name: accountIdentifier
properties.description: The unique identifier for the account.

properties.name: accountIssuer
properties.description: The issuer of this account.

properties.name: accountType
properties.description: The type of account, for instance bank, phone, application, service, etc.

properties.name: expirationTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the validity of the object expires.

properties.name: isActive
properties.description: Indicates whether the network connection is still active.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the Object was last modified.

properties.name: observableCreatedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the observable object being characterized was created. This time pertains to an intrinsic characteristic of the observable object, and would be consistent across independent characterizations or observations of the observable object.

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Specifies the owner of an Observable Object.

properties.name: actionCount
properties.description: The number of times that the action was performed.

properties.name: actionStatus
properties.description: The current state of the action.

properties.name: endTime
properties.description: The time at which performance of the action ended.

properties.name: environment
properties.description: The environment wherein an action occurs.

properties.name: error
properties.description: A characterization of the differences between the expected and the actual performance of the action.

properties.name: instrument
properties.description: The things used to perform an action.

properties.name: location
properties.description: The locations where an action occurs.

properties.name: object
properties.description: The things that the action is performed on/against.

properties.name: participant
properties.description: The supporting (non-primary) performers of an action.

properties.name: performer
properties.description: The primary performer of an action.

properties.name: result
properties.description: The things resulting from performing an action.

properties.name: startTime
properties.description: The time at which performance of the action began.

properties.name: subaction
properties.description: References to other actions that make up part of a larger more complex action.

ActionArgumentFacet.description: An action argument facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a single parameter of an action.

properties.name: argumentName
properties.description: The identifying label of an argument.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value of an action parameter.

ActionEstimationFacet.description: An action estimation facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to decision-focused approximation aspects for an action that may potentially be performed.

properties.name: estimatedCost
properties.description: An estimation of the cost if the action is performed.

properties.name: estimatedEfficacy
properties.description: An estimation of the effectiveness of the action at achieving its objective if the action is performed.

properties.name: estimatedImpact
properties.description: An estimation of the impact if the action is performed.

properties.name: objective
properties.description: The intended purpose for performing the action.

ActionFrequencyFacet.description: An action frequency facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the frequency of occurrence for an action.

properties.name: rate
properties.description: The frequency rate for the occurence of an action.

properties.name: scale
properties.description: The time scale utilized for the frequency rate count for the occurence of an action.

properties.name: trend
properties.description: A characterization of the frequency trend for the occurence of an action.

properties.name: units
properties.description: The units of measure utilized for the frequency rate count for the occurence of an action.

ActionLifecycle.description: An action lifecycle is an action pattern consisting of an ordered set of multiple actions or subordinate action lifecycles.

AndroidDeviceFacet.description: An Android device facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to an Android device. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Android_(operating_system)]

properties.name: androidFingerprint
properties.description: A string that uniquely identifies a build of the Android operating system. [based on https://developer.android.com/reference/android/os/Build#FINGERPRINT]

properties.name: androidID
properties.description: A 64-bit number (expressed as a hexadecimal string), unique to each combination of app-signing key, user, and device. [based on https://developer.android.com/reference/android/provider/Settings.Secure#ANDROID_ID]

properties.name: androidVersion
properties.description: The user-visible version string. E.g., '1.0' or '3.4b5' or 'bananas'. This field is an opaque string. Do not assume that its value has any particular structure or that values of RELEASE from different releases can be somehow ordered. [based on https://developer.android.com/reference/android/os/Build.VERSION#RELEASE]

properties.name: isADBRootEnabled
properties.description: Root access through the Android Debug Bridge (ADB) daemon observed to be enabled. [based on https://developer.android.com/studio/command-line/adb]

properties.name: isSURootEnabled
properties.description: Root access through Linux SU binary observed to be enabled. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rooting_(Android)]

AndroidPhone.description: An android phone is a smart phone that applies the Android mobile operating system.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.description: The median rotation in degrees around a vertical axis of the cell antenna sector accessed.

properties.description: The width of the antenna beam in degrees.

properties.name: signalStrength
properties.description: The strength of the antenna signal.

properties.name: skew
properties.description: The angle in degrees of the radial rotation around its main beam direction.

BluetoothAddressFacet.description: A Bluetooth address facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a Bluetooth standard conformant identifier assigned to a Bluetooth device to enable routing and management of Bluetooth standards conformant communication to or from that device.

properties.name: addressValue
properties.description: The value of an address.

properties.name: displayName
properties.description: Display name specifies the name to display for some entity within a user interface.

BotConfiguration.description: A bot configuration is a set of contextual settings for a software application that runs automated tasks (scripts) over the Internet at a much higher rate than would be possible for a human alone.

properties.name: externalReference
properties.description: Specifies a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: hasFacet
properties.description: Further sets of properties characterizing a concept based on the particular context of the class and of the particular instance of the concept being characterized.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.description: The date and time at which the Object was accessed.

properties.description: The application associated with this object.

properties.name: cookieDomain
properties.description: The domain for which the cookie is stored, for example nfi.minjus.nl.

properties.name: cookieName
properties.description: The name of the cookie.

properties.name: cookiePath
properties.description: String representation of the path of the cookie.

properties.name: expirationTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the validity of the object expires.

properties.name: isSecure
properties.description: Is the cookie secure? If the cookie is secure it cannot be delivered over an unencrypted session such as http.

BuildFacet.description: A build facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a particular version of a software.

properties.name: buildInformation
properties.description: Describes how a particular tool was built.

BuildInformationType.description: A build information type is a grouping of characteristics that describe how a particular version of software was converted from source code to executable code.

properties.name: buildConfiguration
properties.description: How the build utility was configured for a particular build of a particular software.

properties.name: buildID
properties.description: An externally defined unique identifier for a particular build of a software.

properties.name: buildLabel
properties.description: Relevant label for a particular build of a particular software.

properties.name: buildOutputLog
properties.description: The output log of the build process for a software.

properties.name: buildProject
properties.description: The project name of a build of a software.

properties.name: buildScript
properties.description: The actual build script for a particular build of a particular software.

properties.name: buildUtility
properties.description: Identifies the utility used to build a software.

properties.name: buildVersion
properties.description: The appropriate version descriptor of a particular build of a particular software.

properties.name: compilationDate
properties.description: The compilation date for the build of a software.

properties.name: compilers
properties.description: The compilers utilized during a particular build of a particular software.

properties.name: libraries
properties.description: The libraries incorporated into a particular build of a software.

BuildUtilityType.description: A build utility type characterizes the tool used to convert from source code to executable code for a particular version of software.

properties.name: buildUtilityName
properties.description: The informally defined name of the utility used to build a particular software.

properties.name: cpeid
properties.description: Specifies the Common Platform Enumeration identifier for the software.

properties.name: swid
properties.description: Specifies the SWID tag for the software.

Bundle.description: A bundle is a container for a grouping of UCO content with no presumption of shared context.

CapabilityMatrix.description: A capability matrix is a named, versioned collection of ToolCapability assertions that together represent a comprehensive comparison of which digital forensic tools support which applications. A CapabilityMatrix serves as the top-level container for organizing and publishing capability data, enabling forensic labs and DFIR teams to compare tool coverage, identify gaps, plan acquisitions, and track changes over time. The matrix can be serialized as a CASE/UCO-compliant JSON-LD graph and shared across organizations for interoperability.

CellSiteFacet.description: A cell site facet contains the metadata surrounding the cell site.

properties.name: cellSiteCountryCode
properties.description: The country code represents the country of the cell site. For GSM, this is the Mobile Country Code (MCC).

properties.name: cellSiteIdentifier
properties.description: Specifies the unique number used to identify each Cell Site within a location area code.

properties.name: cellSiteLocationAreaCode
properties.description: The location area code is a unique number of current location area of the cell site. A location area is a set of cell site that are grouped together to optimize signalling. For GSM, this is the LAC.

properties.name: cellSiteNetworkCode
properties.description: This code identifies the mobile operator of the cell site. For GSM, this is the Mobile Network Code (MNC) and for CMDA this is the network identifier (NID).

properties.name: cellSiteType
properties.description: Specifies the technology used by the Cell Site (e.g., GSM, CDMA, or LTE).

CharacterDeviceNode.description: A character device node is a UNIX filesystem special file that serves as a conduit to communicate with devices, providing only a serial stream of input or accepting a serial stream of output. Character device nodes are used to apply access rights to the devices and to direct operations on the files to the appropriate device drivers. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unix_file_types]

CompilerType.description: A compiler type is a grouping of characteristics unique to a specific program that translates computer code written in one programming language (the source language) into another language (the target language). Typically a program that translates source code from a high-level programming language to a lower-level language (e.g., assembly language, object code, or machine code) to create an executable program. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compiler]

properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. properties.description: The identity that created a characterization of a concept.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: externalReference
properties.description: Specifies a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: hasFacet
properties.description: Further sets of properties characterizing a concept based on the particular context of the class and of the particular instance of the concept being characterized.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: objectCreatedTime
properties.description: The time at which a characterization of a concept is created. This time pertains to the time of creating the record object, and is not an intrinsic characteristic of the concept.

properties.name: objectMarking
properties.description: Marking definitions to be applied to a particular concept characterization in its entirety.

properties.description: The current state of formality and acceptance for a UCO object.

properties.name: specVersion
properties.description: The version of UCO ontology or subontology specification used to characterize a concept.

DataRangeFacet.description: A data range facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a particular contiguous scope within a block of digital data.

properties.description: The offset at which the start of data can be found, relative to the rangeOffsetType defined.

properties.description: The type of offset defined for the range (e.g., image, file, address).

properties.name: rangeSize
properties.description: The size of the data in bytes.

Dictionary.description: A dictionary is list of (term/key, value) pairs with each term/key having an expectation to exist no more than once.  types:Dictionary alone does not validate this expectation, but validation is available.  For use cases where this expectation must be validated, the subclass types:ProperDictionary should be used instead of types:Dictionary.  For instances where this expectation has been found to be violated, the subclass types:ImproperDictionary should be used instead of types:Dictionary.

properties.name: entry
properties.description: A dictionary entry.

DictionaryEntry.description: A dictionary entry is a single (term/key, value) pair.

properties.name: key
properties.description: A key property of a single dictionary entry.

properties.name: value
properties.description: A specific property value.

Directory.description: A directory is a file system cataloging structure which contains references to other computer files, and possibly other directories. On many computers, directories are known as folders, or drawers, analogous to a workbench or the traditional office filing cabinet. In UNIX a directory is implemented as a special file. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Directory_(computing)]

DiskFacet.description: A disk facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a storage mechanism where data is recorded by various electronic, magnetic, optical, or mechanical changes to a surface layer of one or more rotating disks.

properties.name: diskSize
properties.description: The size of the disk, in bytes.

properties.name: diskType
properties.description: The type of disk being characterized, e.g., removable.

properties.name: freeSpace
properties.description: The amount of free space on the disk, in bytes.

properties.name: partition
properties.description: The partitions that reside on the disk.

DiskPartition.description: A disk partition is a particular managed region on a storage mechanism where data is recorded by various electronic, magnetic, optical, or mechanical changes to a surface layer of one or more rotating disks. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Disk_storage]

DiskPartitionFacet.description: A disk partition facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a particular managed region on a storage mechanism.

properties.name: diskPartitionType
properties.description: Specifies the type of partition being characterized.

properties.name: mountPoint
properties.description: Specifies the mount point of the partition.

properties.name: observableCreatedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the observable object being characterized was created. This time pertains to an intrinsic characteristic of the observable object, and would be consistent across independent characterizations or observations of the observable object.

properties.name: partitionID
properties.description: Specifies the identifier of the partition, as provided by the containing partition table.  This identifier is the index value within the partition table, and is expected to be an incrementing alphanumeric value (numeric in most partition systems), not a GUID or UUID.  Sorting partitions by this index should first attempt to sort a numeric cast of the value.

properties.name: partitionLength
properties.description: Specifies the length of the partition, in bytes.

properties.name: partitionOffset
properties.description: Specifies the starting offset of the partition, in bytes.

properties.name: spaceLeft
properties.description: Specifies the amount of space left on the partition, in bytes.

properties.name: spaceUsed
properties.description: Specifies the amount of space used on the partition, in bytes.

properties.name: totalSpace
properties.description: Specifies the total amount of space available on the partition, in bytes.

DomainName.description: A domain name is an identification string that defines a realm of administrative autonomy, authority or control within the Internet. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Domain_name]

properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. EmailMessageFacet.description: An email message facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a message that is an instance of an electronic mail correspondence conformant to the internet message format described in RFC 5322 and related RFCs.

properties.name: application
properties.description: The application associated with this object.

properties.name: bodyMultipart
properties.description: A list of the MIME parts that make up the email body. This field MAY only be used if isMultipart is true.

properties.name: categories
properties.description: Categories applied to the object.

properties.name: from
properties.description: The phone number of the initiating party.

properties.name: inReplyTo
properties.description: One of more unique identifiers for identifying the email(s) this email is a reply to.

properties.name: labels
properties.description: Named and colored label.

properties.name: messageID
properties.description: An unique identifier for the message.

properties.name: modifiedTime

properties.name: priority
properties.description: The priority of the email.

properties.name: receivedTime

properties.name: references
properties.description: A list of email message identifiers this email relates to.

properties.name: sentTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the message sent.

properties.name: subject
properties.description: The subject of the email.

properties.name: to
properties.description: The receiver's phone number.

EncodedStreamFacet.description: An encoded stream facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the conversion of a body of data content from one form to another form.

EncryptedStreamFacet.description: An encrypted stream facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the conversion of a body of data content from one form to another obfuscated form in such a way that reversing the conversion to obtain the original data form can only be accomplished through possession and use of a specific key.

EnvironmentVariable.description: An environment variable is a grouping of characteristics unique to a dynamic-named value that can affect the way running processes will behave on a computer. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Environment_variable]

EventRecordFacet.description: An event record facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to something that happens in a digital context (e.g., operating system events).

properties.name: account
properties.description: Specifies the account referenced in an event log entry or used to run the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381228(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: application
properties.description: The application associated with this object.

properties.name: cyberAction
properties.description: The action taken in response to the event.

properties.name: eventRecordDevice
properties.description: The device on which the log entry was generated.

properties.name: eventRecordID
properties.description: The identifier of the event record.

properties.name: eventRecordRaw
properties.description: The complete raw content of the event record.

properties.name: eventRecordServiceName
properties.description: The service that generated the event record. A single application can have multiple services generating event records.

properties.name: eventRecordText
properties.description: The textual representation of the event.

properties.name: eventType
properties.description: The type of the event, for example 'information', 'warning' or 'error'.

properties.name: observableCreatedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the observable object being characterized was created. This time pertains to an intrinsic characteristic of the observable object, and would be consistent across independent characterizations or observations of the observable object.

EventsFacet.description: Events is a grouping of characteristics unique to information related to specific relevant things that happen in the lifetime of an entity.

Examiner.description: Examiner is a role involved in providing scientific evaluations of evidence that are used to aid law enforcement investigations and court cases.

ExtInodeFacet.description: An extInode facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a file system object (file, directory, etc.) conformant to the extended file system (EXT or related derivations) specification.

properties.name: extDeletionTime
properties.description: Specifies the time at which the file represented by an Inode was 'deleted'.

properties.name: extFileType
properties.description: Specifies the EXT file type (FIFO, Directory, Regular file, Symbolic link, etc) for the Inode.

properties.name: extFlags
properties.description: Specifies user flags to further protect (limit its use and modification) the file represented by an Inode.

properties.name: extHardLinkCount
properties.description: Specifies a count of how many hard links point to an Inode.

properties.name: extInodeChangeTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the file Inode metadata was last modified.

properties.name: extInodeID
properties.description: Specifies a single Inode identifier.

properties.name: extPermissions
properties.description: Specifies the read/write/execute permissions for the file represented by an EXT Inode.

properties.name: extSGID
properties.description: Specifies the group ID for the file represented by an Inode.

properties.name: extSUID
properties.description: Specifies the user ID that 'owns' the file represented by an Inode.

ExternalReference.description: Characteristics of a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: definingContext
properties.description: A description of the context relevant to the definition of a particular external reference identifier.

properties.name: externalIdentifier
properties.description: An identifier for some information defined external to the UCO context.

properties.name: referenceURL
properties.description: A URL for some information defined external to the UCO context.

ExtractedString.description: An extracted string is a grouping of characteristics unique to a series of characters pulled from an observable object.

properties.name: byteStringValue
properties.description: Specifies the raw, byte-string representation of the extracted string.

properties.name: encoding
properties.description: The encoding method used for the extracted string.

properties.name: englishTranslation
properties.description: Specifies the English translation of the string, if it is not written in English.

properties.name: language
properties.description:
Specifies the language the string is written in, e.g. English.
          For consistency, it is strongly recommended to use the ISO 639-2 language code, if available. Please see http://www.loc.gov/standards/iso639-2/php/code_list.php for a list of ISO 639-2 codes.

properties.name: length
properties.description: Specifies the length, in characters, of the extracted string.

properties.name: stringValue
properties.description: Specifies the actual value of the extracted string.

ExtractedStringsFacet.description: An extracted strings facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to one or more sequences of characters pulled from an observable object.

Facet.description: A facet is a grouping of characteristics singularly unique to a particular inherent aspect of a UCO domain object.

File.description: A file is a computer resource for recording data discretely on a computer storage device.

FileFacet.description: A file facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the storage of a file (computer resource for recording data discretely in a computer storage device) on a file system (process that manages how and where data on a storage device is stored, accessed and managed). [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/Computer_file and https://www.techopedia.com/definition/5510/file-system]

properties.name: accessedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the Object was accessed.

properties.name: allocationStatus
properties.description: The allocation status of a file.

properties.name: extension
properties.description: The file name extension: everything after the last dot. Not present if the file has no dot in its name.

properties.name: fileName
properties.description: Specifies the name associated with a file in a file system.

properties.name: filePath
properties.description: Specifies the file path for the location of a file within a filesystem.

properties.name: isDirectory
properties.description: Specifies whether a file entry represents a directory.

properties.name: metadataChangeTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the file metadata was last modified.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the Object was last modified.

properties.name: observableCreatedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the observable object being characterized was created. This time pertains to an intrinsic characteristic of the observable object, and would be consistent across independent characterizations or observations of the observable object.

properties.name: sizeInBytes
properties.description: The size of the data in bytes.

FilePermissionsFacet.description: A file permissions facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the access rights (e.g., view, change, navigate, execute) of a file on a file system.

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Specifies the owner of an Observable Object.

FileSystem.description: A file system is the process that manages how and where data on a storage medium is stored, accessed and managed. [based on https://www.techopedia.com/definition/5510/file-system]

properties.name: fileSystemType
properties.description: The specific type of a file system.

FileSystemObject.description: A file system object is an informational object represented and managed within a file system.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

ForumPost.description: A forum post is message submitted by a user account to an online forum where the message content (and typically metadata including who posted it and when) is viewable by any party with viewing permissions on the forum.

ForumPrivateMessage.description: A forum private message (aka PM or DM (direct message)) is a one-to-one message from one specific user account to another specific user account on an online form where transmission is managed by the online forum platform and the message is only viewable by the parties directly involved.

FragmentFacet.description: A fragment facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to an individual piece of the content of a file.

GPSCoordinatesFacet.description: A GPS coordinates facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the expression of quantified dilution of precision (DOP) for an asserted set of geolocation coordinates typically associated with satellite navigation such as the Global Positioning System (GPS).

properties.name: hdop
properties.description: The horizontal dilution of precision of the GPS location.

properties.name: pdop
properties.description: The positional (3D) dilution of precision of the GPS location.

properties.name: tdop
properties.description: The temporal dilution of precision of the GPS location.

properties.name: vdop
properties.description: The vertical dilution of precision of the GPS location.

GUI.description: A GUI is a graphical user interface that allows users to interact with electronic devices through graphical icons and audio indicators such as primary notation, instead of text-based user interfaces, typed command labels or text navigation. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graphical_user_interface]

GlobalFlagType.description: A global flag type is a grouping of characteristics unique to the Windows systemwide global variable named NtGlobalFlag that enables various internal debugging, tracing, and validation support in the operating system. [based on "Windows Global Flags, Chapter 3: System Mechanisms of Windows Internals by Solomon, Russinovich, and Ionescu]

properties.name: abbreviation
properties.description: The abbreviation of a global flag. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/hardware/ff549646(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: destination
properties.description: The destination of a global flag. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/hardware/ff549646(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: hexadecimalValue
properties.description: The hexadecimal value of a global flag. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/hardware/ff549646(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: symbolicName
properties.description: The symbolic name of a global flag. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/hardware/ff549646(v=vs.85).aspx.

GranularMarking.description: A granular marking is a grouping of characteristics unique to specification of marking definitions (restrictions, permissions, and other guidance for how data can be used and shared) that apply to particular portions of a particular UCO object.

properties.name: contentSelectors
properties.description:
Explicit specification of exactly which portions of a UCO object to apply marking definitions to.
  Specific syntax for how to specify the UCO object portions is dependent on the particular syntactic serialization implementation (XML, JSON, etc.) of UCO and MUST be explicitly specified in a separate binding specification for that syntactic serialization implementation (e.g. a UCO XML Binding Specification).

properties.name: marking
properties.description: Represents specific marking definitions to be applied to UCO data.

Grouping.description: A grouping is a compilation of referenced UCO content with a shared context.

HTTPConnectionFacet.description: An HTTP connection facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to portions of a network connection that are conformant to the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) standard.

properties.name: httpMesageBodyLength
properties.description: Specifies the length of an HTTP message body in bytes.

properties.name: httpMessageBodyData
properties.description: Specifies the data contained in an HTTP message body.

properties.name: httpRequestHeader
properties.description: Specifies all of the HTTP header fields that may be found in the HTTP client request

properties.name: requestMethod
properties.description: Specifies the HTTP method portion of the HTTP request line, as a lowercase string.

properties.name: requestValue
properties.description: Specifies the value (typically a resource path) portion of the HTTP request line.

properties.name: requestVersion
properties.description: Specifies the HTTP version portion of the HTTP request line, as a lowercase string.

Hash.description: A hash is a grouping of characteristics unique to the result of applying a mathematical algorithm that maps data of arbitrary size to a bit string (the 'hash') and is a one-way function, that is, a function which is practically infeasible to invert. This is commonly used for integrity checking of data. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cryptographic_hash_function]

properties.name: hashMethod
properties.description: A particular cryptographic hashing method (e.g., MD5).

properties.name: hashValue
properties.description: A cryptographic hash value.

Hostname.description: A hostname is a label that is assigned to a device connected to a computer network and that is used to identify the device in various forms of electronic communication, such as the World Wide Web. A hostname may be a domain name, if it is properly organized into the domain name system. A domain name may be a hostname if it has been assigned to an Internet host and associated with the host's IP address. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hostname]

properties.description: Specifies the ICMP code byte.

properties.name: icmpType
properties.description: Specifies the ICMP type byte.

IComHandlerActionType.description: An IComHandler action type is a grouping of characteristics unique to a Windows Task-related action that fires a Windows COM handler (smart code in the client address space that can optimize calls between a client and server). [based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/taskschd/comhandleraction]

properties.name: comClassID
properties.description: Specifies the ID of the COM action. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380613(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: comData
properties.description: Specifies the data associated with the COM handler. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380613(v=vs.85).aspx.

IExecActionType.description: An IExec action type is a grouping of characteristics unique to an action that executes a command-line operation on a Windows operating system. [based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/taskschd/nn-taskschd-iexecaction?redirectedfrom=MSDN]

properties.name: execArguments
properties.description: Specifies the arguments associated with the command-line operation launched by the action. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380715(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: execProgramHashes
properties.description: Specifies the hashes of the executable file launched by the action.

properties.name: execProgramPath
properties.description: Specifies the path to the executable file launched by the action. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380715(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: execWorkingDirectory
properties.description: Specifies the directory that contains either the executable file or the files that are used by the executable file launched by the action. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380715(v=vs.85).aspx.

IPAddress.description: An IP address is an Internet Protocol (IP) standards conformant identifier assigned to a device to enable routing and management of IP standards conformant communication to or from that device.

properties.description: References to other actions that make up part of a larger more complex action.

Junction.description: A junction is a specific NTFS (New Technology File System) reparse point to redirect a directory access to another directory which can be on the same volume or another volume. A junction is similar to a directory symbolic link but may differ on whether they are processed on the local system or on the remote file server. [based on https://jp-andre.pagesperso-orange.fr/junctions.html]

LanguagesFacet.description: Languages is a grouping of characteristics unique to specific syntactically and grammatically standardized forms of communication (human or computer) in which an entity has proficiency (comprehends, speaks, reads, or writes).

Laptop.description: A laptop, laptop computer, or notebook computer is a small, portable personal computer with a screen and alphanumeric keyboard. These typically have a clam shell form factor with the screen mounted on the inside of the upper lid and the keyboard on the inside of the lower lid, although 2-in-1 PCs with a detachable keyboard are often marketed as laptops or as having a laptop mode. (Devices categorized by their manufacturer as a Laptop)

properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. MemoryFacet.description: A memory facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a particular region of temporary information storage (e.g., RAM (random access memory), ROM (read only memory)) on a digital device.

properties.name: blockType
properties.description: The blockType property specifies the block type of a particular memory object.

properties.name: isInjected
properties.description: The isInjected property specifies whether or not the particular memory object has had data/code injected into it by another process.

properties.name: isMapped
properties.description: The isMapped property specifies whether or not the particular memory object has been assigned a byte-for-byte correlation with some portion of a file or file-like resource.

properties.name: isProtected
properties.description: The isProtected property specifies whether or not the particular memory object is protected (read/write only from the process that allocated it).

properties.name: isVolatile
properties.description: The isVolatile property specifies whether or not the particular memory object is volatile.

properties.name: regionEndAddress
properties.description: The regionEndAddress property specifies the ending address of the particular memory region.

properties.name: regionSize
properties.description: The regionSize property specifies the size of the particular memory region, in bytes.

properties.name: regionStartAddress
properties.description: The regionStartAddress property specifies the starting address of the particular memory region.

MftRecordFacet.description: An MFT record facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the details of a single file as managed in an NTFS (new technology filesystem) master file table (which is a collection of information about all files on an NTFS filesystem). [based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/devnotes/master-file-table]

properties.name: mftFileID
properties.description: Specifies the record number for the file within an NTFS Master File Table.

properties.name: mftFileNameAccessedTime
properties.description: The access date and time recorded in an MFT entry $File_Name attribute.

properties.name: mftFileNameCreatedTime
properties.description: The creation date and time recorded in an MFT entry $File_Name attribute.

properties.name: mftFileNameLength
properties.description: Specifies the length of an NTFS file name, in unicode characters.

properties.name: mftFileNameModifiedTime
properties.description: The modification date and time recorded in an MFT entry $File_Name attribute.

properties.name: mftFileNameRecordChangeTime
properties.description: The metadata modification date and time recorded in an MFT entry $File_Name attribute.

properties.name: mftFlags
properties.description: Specifies basic permissions for the file (Read-Only, Hidden, Archive, Compressed, etc.).

properties.name: mftParentID
properties.description: Specifies the record number within an NTFS Master File Table for parent directory of the file.

properties.name: mftRecordChangeTime
properties.description: The date and time at which an NTFS file metadata was last modified.

properties.name: ntfsHardLinkCount
properties.description: Specifies the number of directory entries that reference an NTFS file record.

properties.name: ntfsOwnerID
properties.description: Specifies the identifier of the file owner, from the security index.

properties.name: ntfsOwnerSID
properties.description: Specifies the security ID (key in the $SII Index and $SDS DataStream in the file $Secure) for an NTFS file.

MimePartType.description: A mime part type is a grouping of characteristics unique to a component of a multi-part email body.

MobileAccount.description: A mobile account is an arrangement with an entity to enable and control the provision of some capability or service on a portable computing device. [based on https://www.lexico.com/definition/mobile_device]

properties.name: IMSI
properties.description: An International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) is a unique identification associated with all GSM and UMTS network mobile phone users. It is stored as a 64-bit field in the SIM inside the phone and is sent by the phone to the network.

properties.name: MSISDN
properties.description: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN) is a number used to identify a mobile phone number internationally. MSISDN is defined by the E.164 numbering plan. This number includes a country code and a National Destination Code which identifies the subscriber's operator.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

Mutex.description: A mutex is a mechanism that enforces limits on access to a resource when there are many threads of execution. A mutex is designed to enforce a mutual exclusion concurrency control policy, and with a variety of possible methods there exists multiple unique implementations for different applications. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lock_(computer_science)]

NTFSFileFacet.description: An NTFS file facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a file on an NTFS (new technology filesystem) file system.

properties.name: entryID
properties.description: A unique identifier for the file within the filesystem.

NTFSFilePermissionsFacet.description: An NTFS file permissions facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the access rights (e.g., view, change, navigate, execute) of a file on an NTFS (new technology filesystem) file system.

NamedPipe.description: A named pipe is a mechanism for FIFO (first-in-first-out) inter-process communication. It is persisted as a filesystem object (that can be deleted like any other file), can be written to or read from by any process and exists beyond the lifespan of any process interacting with it (unlike simple anonymous pipes). [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Named_pipe]

NetworkConnectionFacet.description: A network connection facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a connection (complete or attempted) accross a digital network (a group of two or more computer systems linked together). [based on https://www.webopedia.com/TERM/N/network.html]

properties.name: destinationPort
properties.description: Specifies the destination port used in the connection, as an integer in the range of 0 - 65535.

properties.name: dst
properties.description: Specifies the destination(s) of the network connection.

properties.name: isActive
properties.description: Indicates whether the network connection is still active.

properties.name: protocols
properties.description: Specifies the protocols involved in the network connection, along with their corresponding state.

properties.name: sourcePort
properties.description: Specifies the source port used in the connection, as an integer in the range of 0 - 65535.

properties.name: src
properties.description: Specifies the source(s) of the network connection.

NetworkInterfaceFacet.description: A network interface facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a software or hardware interface between two pieces of equipment or protocol layers in a computer network.

properties.name: adapterName
properties.description: Specifies the name of the network adapter used by the network interface.

properties.name: dhcpLeaseExpires
properties.description: Specifies the date/time that the DHCP lease obtained on the network interface expires.

properties.name: dhcpLeaseObtained
properties.description: Specifies the date/time that the DHCP lease was obtained on the network interface.

properties.name: dhcpServer
properties.description: Specifies the list of DHCP server IP Addresses used by the network interface.

properties.name: ip
properties.description: Specifies the list of IP addresses used by the network interface.

properties.name: ipGateway
properties.description: Specifies the list of IP Gateway IP Addresses used by the network interface.

properties.name: macAddress
properties.description: Specifies the MAC or hardware address of the physical network card.

NetworkProtocol.description: A network protocol is an established set of structured rules that determine how data is transmitted between different devices in the same network. Essentially, it allows connected devices to communicate with each other, regardless of any differences in their internal processes, structure or design. [based on https://www.comptia.org/content/guides/what-is-a-network-protocol]

properties.description: The number of times that the action was performed.

properties.description: The time at which performance of the action ended.

properties.description: The primary performer of an action.

properties.name: subaction
properties.description: References to other actions that make up part of a larger more complex action.

ObservableObject.description: An observable object is a grouping of characteristics unique to a distinct article or unit within the digital domain.

properties.name: createdBy
properties.description: The identity that created a characterization of a concept.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: externalReference
properties.description: Specifies a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: hasFacet
properties.description: Further sets of properties characterizing a concept based on the particular context of the class and of the particular instance of the concept being characterized.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.name: pdfCreationDate
properties.description: The PDF CreationDate property is defined in ISO 32000-1:2008 as 'The date and time the document was created, in human-readable form' (Table 317).  As a UCO property, its value is converted to xsd:dateTime.

properties.name: pdfModDate
properties.description: The PDF ModDate property is defined in ISO 32000-1:2008 as 'The date and time the document was most recently modified, in human-readable form' (Table 317).  As a UCO property, its value is converted to xsd:dateTime.

The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

ProcessFacet.description: A process facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to an instance of a computer program executed on an operating system.

properties.name: arguments
properties.description: A list of arguments utilized in initiating the process.

properties.name: creatorUser
properties.description: The user that created/owns the process.

properties.name: environmentVariables
properties.description: A list of environment variables associated with the process.

properties.name: exitStatus
properties.description: A small number passed from the process to the parent process when it has finished executing. In general, 0 indicates successful termination, any other number indicates a failure.

properties.name: exitTime
properties.description: The time at which the process exited.

properties.name: isHidden
properties.description: The isHidden property specifies whether the process is hidden or not.

properties.name: observableCreatedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the observable object being characterized was created. This time pertains to an intrinsic characteristic of the observable object, and would be consistent across independent characterizations or observations of the observable object.

properties.name: parent
properties.description: The process that created this process.

properties.name: pid
properties.description: The Process ID, or PID, of the process.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Specifies a list of statuses for a given Whois entry.

ProcessThread.description: A process thread is the smallest sequence of programmed instructions that can be managed independently by a scheduler on a computer, which is typically a part of the operating system. It is a component of a process. Multiple threads can exist within one process, executing concurrently and sharing resources such as memory, while different processes do not share these resources. In particular, the threads of a process share its executable code and the values of its dynamically allocated variables and non-thread-local global variables at any given time. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thread_(computing)]

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: contactAddress
properties.description: Contact address specifies information characterizing a geolocation address of a contact entity.

properties.name: contactEmail
properties.description: Contact email specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by email.

properties.name: contactMessaging
properties.description: Contact messaging specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by digital messaging.

properties.name: contactPhone
properties.description: Contact phone specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by telephone.

properties.name: contactURL
properties.description: Contact URL specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by Uniform Resource Locator (URL).

properties.name: displayName
properties.description: Display name specifies the name to display for some entity within a user interface.

properties.name: profileAccount
properties.description: Specifies the online service account associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileCreated
properties.description: Specifies the date and time the profile was created.

properties.name: profileIdentity
properties.description: Specifies the identity associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileLanguage
properties.description: Specifies the language associated with the profile. When present, it MUST be a language code conformant to RFC 5646/BCP47.

properties.name: profileService
properties.description: Specifies the online service associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileWebsite
properties.description: Specifies the website URL associated with the profile.

ProperDictionary.description: A proper dictionary is list of (term/key, value) pairs with each term/key existing no more than once.

properties.name: entry
properties.description: A dictionary entry.

PropertiesEnumeratedEffectFacet.description: A properties enumerated effect facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the effects of actions upon observable objects where a characteristic of the observable object is enumerated. An example of this would be startup parameters for a process.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: Specifies the properties that were enumerated as a result of the action on the observable object.

PropertyReadEffectFacet.description: A properties read effect facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the effects of actions upon observable objects where a characteristic is read from an observable object. An example of this would be the current running state of a process.

properties.name: propertyName
properties.description: Specifies the Name of the property being read.

ProtocolConverter.description: A protocol converter is a device that converts from one protocol to another (e.g. SD to USB, SATA to USB, etc.

ReleaseToMarking.description: A release-to marking is a grouping of characteristics unique to the expression of data marking definitions (restrictions, permissions, and other guidance for how data can be used and shared) to convey details of authorized persons and/or organizations to which to the associated content may be released. The existence of the Release-To marking restricts access to ONLY those identities explicitly listed, regardless of whether another data marking exists that allows sharing with other members of the community.

properties.name: authorizedIdentities
properties.description: Specifies the identities that are authorized to access the data to which the data marking is associated.  The list of authorized identities are represented as UUIDs.

properties.name: definitionType
properties.description: Specifies the Marking Model for a Marking Definition.

ReparsePoint.description: A reparse point is a type of NTFS (New Technology File System) object which is an optional attribute of files and directories meant to define some sort of preprocessing before accessing the said file or directory. For instance reparse points can be used to redirect access to files which have been moved to long term storage so that some application would retrieve them and make them directly accessible. A reparse point contains a reparse tag and data that are interpreted by a filesystem filter identified by the tag. [based on https://jp-andre.pagesperso-orange.fr/junctions.html ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NTFS_reparse_point]

SIMCardFacet.description: A SIM card facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a subscriber identification module card intended to securely store the international mobile subscriber identity (IMSI) number and its related key, which are used to identify and authenticate subscribers on mobile telephony devices (such as mobile phones and computers). [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SIM_card]

properties.name: ICCID
properties.description: Integrated circuit card identifier (http://www.itu.int/).

properties.name: IMSI
properties.description: An International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) is a unique identification associated with all GSM and UMTS network mobile phone users. It is stored as a 64-bit field in the SIM inside the phone and is sent by the phone to the network.

properties.name: PIN
properties.description: Personal Identification Number (PIN).

properties.name: PUK
properties.description: Personal Unlocking Key (PUK) to unlock the SIM card.

properties.name: SIMForm
properties.description: The form of SIM card such as SIM, Micro SIM, Nano SIM.

properties.name: SIMType
properties.description: The type of SIM card such as SIM, USIM, UICC.

properties.name: carrier
properties.description: Telecommunications service provider that sold the SIM card.

properties.name: storageCapacityInBytes
properties.description: The number of bytes that can be stored on a SIM card.

properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

Socket.description: A socket is a special file used for inter-process communication, which enables communication between two processes. In addition to sending data, processes can send file descriptors across a Unix domain socket connection using the sendmsg() and recvmsg() system calls. SoftwareFacet.description: A software facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a software program (a definitively scoped instance of a collection of data or computer instructions that tell the computer how to work). [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software]

properties.name: cpeid
properties.description: Specifies the Common Platform Enumeration identifier for the software.

properties.name: language
properties.description:
Specifies the language the string is written in, e.g. English.
          For consistency, it is strongly recommended to use the ISO 639-2 language code, if available. Please see http://www.loc.gov/standards/iso639-2/php/code_list.php for a list of ISO 639-2 codes.

properties.name: swid
properties.description: Specifies the SWID tag for the software.

StateChangeEffectFacet.description: A state change effect facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the effects of actions upon observable objects where a state of the observable object is changed.

properties.name: newObject
properties.description: Specifies the observable object and its properties as they are after the state change effect occurred.

properties.name: oldObject
properties.description: Specifies the observable object and its properties as they were before the state change effect occurred.

StatementMarking.description: A statement marking is a grouping of characteristics unique to the expression of data marking definitions (restrictions, permissions, and other guidance for how data can be used and shared) to convey details of a textual marking statement, (e.g., copyright) whose semantic meaning should apply to the associated content. Statement markings are generally not machine-readable. An example of this would be a simple marking to apply copyright information, such as 'Copyright 2014 Acme Inc.'.

properties.name: definitionType
properties.description: Specifies the Marking Model for a Marking Definition.

properties.name: statement
properties.description: Specifies the statement to apply to the structure for which the Marking is to be applied.

properties.description: The number of times that the action was performed.

properties.name: actionStatus
properties.description: The current state of the action.

properties.name: endTime
properties.description: The time at which performance of the action ended.

properties.name: environment
properties.description: The environment wherein an action occurs.

properties.name: error
properties.description: A characterization of the differences between the expected and the actual performance of the action.

properties.name: instrument
properties.description: The things used to perform an action.

properties.name: location
properties.description: The locations where an action occurs.

properties.name: object
properties.description: The things that the action is performed on/against.

properties.name: participant
properties.description: The supporting (non-primary) performers of an action.

properties.name: performer
properties.description: The primary performer of an action.

properties.name: result
properties.description: The things resulting from performing an action.

properties.name: startTime
properties.description: The time at which performance of the action began.

properties.name: subaction
properties.description: References to other actions that make up part of a larger more complex action.

properties.name: phase
properties.description: The ordered set of actions or sub action-lifecycles that represent the action lifecycle.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.name: sourceFlags

properties.name: recordFieldIsNull
properties.description: Whether the specified database record field is null.

properties.name: recordFieldName
properties.description: A field name of a database record value being identified.

properties.name: recordFieldValue

properties.name: recordRowID

properties.name: tableName

properties.name: tableSchema

Tablet.description: A tablet is a mobile computer that is primarily operated by touching the screen. (Devices categorized by their manufacturer as a Tablet)

TaskActionType.description: A task action type is a grouping of characteristics for a scheduled action to be completed.

properties.name: actionID
properties.description: Specifies the user-defined identifier for the action. This identifier is used by the Task Scheduler for logging purposes. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380590(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: actionType
properties.description: Specifies the type of the action. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380596(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: iComHandlerAction
properties.description: Specifies the data associated with the task action-fired COM handler.

properties.name: iEmailAction
properties.description: Specifies an action that sends an e-mail, which in this context refers to actual email message sent. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380693(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: iExecAction
properties.description: Specifies an action that executes a command-line operation. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa380715(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: iShowMessageAction
properties.description: Specifies an action that shows a message box when a task is activated. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381302(v=vs.85).aspx.

TermsOfUseMarking.description: A terms of use marking is a grouping of characteristics unique to the expression of data marking definitions (restrictions, permissions, and other guidance for how data can be used and shared) to convey details of a textual statement specifying the Terms of Use (that is, the conditions under which the content may be shared, applied, or otherwise used) of the marked content. An example of this would be used to communicate a simple statement, such as 'Acme Inc. is not responsible for the content of this file'.

properties.name: definitionType
properties.description: Specifies the Marking Model for a Marking Definition.

properties.name: termsOfUse
properties.description: Specifies the terms of use that apply to the structure for which the Marking is to be applied.

Thread.description: A semi-ordered array of items, that can be present in multiple copies.  Implemetation of a UCO Thread is similar to a Collections Ontology List, except a Thread may fork and merge - that is, one of its members may have two or more direct successors, and two or more direct predecessors.

ThreadItem.description: A ThreadItem is a member of a thread.

ToolCapability.description: A tool capability is a formal assertion that a specific digital forensic tool can successfully parse, extract, or decode data from a specific application on one or more device platforms. Each ToolCapability instance links exactly one forensic tool (uco-tool:Tool) to exactly one target application (uco-observable:ObservableObject with an ApplicationFacet), and may additionally specify the platforms supported, the types of observables the tool can extract, the tool version tested, whether the capability has been independently verified, and when it was last tested. ToolCapability instances are the building blocks of a capability comparison matrix used to evaluate forensic tool coverage across the application and platform landscape.

properties.name: createdBy
properties.description: The identity that created a characterization of a concept.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: externalReference
properties.description: Specifies a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: specVersion
properties.description: The version of UCO ontology or subontology specification used to characterize a concept.

properties.name: tag
properties.description: A generic tag/label.

properties.name: application
properties.description: Identifies the target application (an instance of uco-observable:ObservableObject, typically with an ApplicationFacet) whose data can be parsed by the forensic tool. For example, a ToolCapability asserting that a tool can parse a specific application would link to the ObservableObject representing that application via this property.

properties.name: isVerified
properties.description: A boolean flag indicating whether this tool capability has been independently verified through controlled testing (e.g., running the tool against a known test image containing the target application's data and confirming successful extraction). A value of true means the capability has been validated; false or omission means it is unverified or self-reported.

properties.name: lastTestedDate
properties.description: The date and time when this capability was most recently tested or verified. This is important for tracking currency of the capability assertion, since applications frequently update their data storage formats and forensic tools must keep pace.

properties.name: parsedObservableType
properties.description: A category of observable data (e.g., 'messages', 'contacts', 'call logs', 'media files', 'calendar events', 'location data', 'browser history') that the forensic tool can extract from the target application. Multiple values indicate the tool can parse several observable categories from the same application.

properties.name: supportedPlatform
properties.description: A device platform or operating system (e.g., 'Android', 'iOS', 'Windows', 'macOS', 'Linux') on which the forensic tool can successfully parse data from the target application. Multiple values are expected when the tool supports the same application across multiple platforms.

properties.name: tool
properties.description: Identifies the digital forensic tool (an instance of uco-tool:Tool) that possesses this capability. For example, a ToolCapability asserting that a forensic tool can parse a messaging application would link to the Tool instance via this property.

properties.name: toolVersion
properties.description: The specific version string of the forensic tool that was tested or verified to support this capability. Because forensic tool capabilities change between releases (new application parsers are added, existing ones may break), recording the tested version is critical for reproducibility and auditability.

TriggerType.description: A trigger type is a grouping of characterizes unique to a set of criteria that, when met, starts the execution of a task within a Windows operating system. [based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/taskschd/task-triggers]

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.description: Specifies whether the trigger is enabled.

properties.name: triggerBeginTime
properties.description: Specifies the date/time that the trigger is activated.

properties.name: triggerDelay
properties.description: Specifies the delay that takes place between when the task is registered and when the task is started.

properties.name: triggerEndTime
properties.description: Specifies the date/time that the trigger is deactivated.

properties.name: triggerFrequency
properties.description: Specifies the frequency at which the trigger repeats.

properties.name: triggerMaxRunTime
properties.description: The maximum amount of time that the task launched by the trigger is allowed to run. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa383868(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: triggerSessionChangeType
properties.description: Specifies the type of Terminal Server session change that would trigger a task launch. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381298(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: triggerType
properties.description: Specifies the type of the task trigger.

TwitterProfileFacet.description: A twitter profile facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to an explicit digital representation of identity and characteristics of the owner of a single Twitter user account. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/User_profile]

properties.name: favoritesCount
properties.description: Specifies the number of times that this profile has favorited a tweet.

properties.name: followersCount
properties.description: Specifies the followers count associated with the twitter profile.

properties.name: friendsCount
properties.description: Specifies the friends count associated with the twitter profile.

properties.name: listedCount
properties.description: Specifies the number of public lists that this profile is associated with.

properties.name: profileBackgroundHash
properties.description: Specifies hashes of the background associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileBackgroundLocation
properties.description: Specifies the network location of the background associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileBannerHash
properties.description: Specifies hashes of the banner associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileBannerLocation
properties.description: Specifies the network location of the banner associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileImageHash
properties.description: Specifies hashes of the profile image associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileImageLocation
properties.description: Specifies the network location of the profile image associated with the profile.

properties.name: profileIsProtected
properties.description: Specifies whether the twitter profile is protected.

properties.name: profileIsVerified
properties.description: Specifies whether the twitter profile is verified.

properties.name: statusesCount
properties.description: Specifies the number of tweets that this profile has issued.

properties.name: twitterHandle
properties.description: Specifies the twitter handle associated with the profile.

properties.name: twitterId
properties.description: Specifies the twitter id associated with the profile.

properties.name: userLocationString
properties.description: Specifies the user-provided location string associated with the profile.

properties.name: ruid
properties.description: Specifies the real user ID, which represents the Unix user who created the process.

UNIXVolumeFacet.description: A UNIX volume facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a single accessible storage area (volume) with a single UNIX file system. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Volume_(computing)]

properties.name: mountPoint
properties.description: Specifies the mount point of the partition.

properties.name: options
properties.description: Specifies any options used when mounting the volume.
