URLFacet.description: A URL facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a uniform resource locator (URL) acting as a resolvable address to a particular WWW (World Wide Web) accessible resource.

properties.name: fragment
properties.description: Fragment pointing to a specific part in the resource.

properties.name: fullValue
properties.description: The full string value of the URL.

properties.name: host
properties.description: Domain name or IP address where the resource is located.

properties.name: password
properties.description: Specifies an authentication password.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Specifies the location of one object within another containing object.

properties.name: port
properties.description: Port on which communication takes place.

properties.name: query
properties.description: Query passed to the resource.

properties.name: scheme
properties.description: Identifies the type of URL.

properties.name: userName
properties.description: Username used to authenticate to this resource.

URLHistory.description: A URL history characterizes the stored URL history for a particular web browser

URLHistoryEntry.description: A URL history entry is a grouping of characteristics unique to the properties of a single URL history entry for a particular browser.

properties.name: browserUserProfile
properties.description: Specifies the web browser user profile for which the URL history entry was created.

properties.name: expirationTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the validity of the object expires.

properties.name: firstVisit
properties.description: Specifies the date/time that the URL referred to by the URL field was first visited.

properties.name: hostname
properties.description: Specifies the hostname of the system.

properties.name: keywordSearchTerm
properties.description: Specifies a string representing a keyword search term contained within the URL field.

properties.name: lastVisit
properties.description: Specifies the date/time that the URL referred to by the URL field was last visited.

properties.name: manuallyEnteredCount
properties.description: Specifies the number of times the URL referred to by the URL field was manually entered into the browser's address field by the user. This field is only applicable for URL history entries generated by Google's Chrome browser.

properties.name: pageTitle
properties.description: Specifies the title of a web page.

properties.name: referrerUrl
properties.description: Specifies the origination point (i.e., URL) of a URL request.

properties.name: url
properties.description: Specifies a URL associated with a particular observable object or facet.

properties.name: visitCount
properties.description: Specifies the number of times a URL has been visited by a particular web browser.

URLHistoryFacet.description: A URL history facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the stored URL history for a particular web browser

properties.name: browserInformation
properties.description: Specifies information about the particular Web Browser.

properties.name: urlHistoryEntry
properties.description: Specifies a URL history record stored in the browser's history.

URLVisit.description: A URL visit characterizes the properties of a visit of a URL within a particular browser.

properties.name: createdBy
properties.description: The identity that created a characterization of a concept.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: externalReference
properties.description: Specifies a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: hasFacet
properties.description: Further sets of properties characterizing a concept based on the particular context of the class and of the particular instance of the concept being characterized.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: objectCreatedTime
properties.description: The time at which a characterization of a concept is created. This time pertains to the time of creating the record object, and is not an intrinsic characteristic of the concept.

properties.name: objectMarking
properties.description: Marking definitions to be applied to a particular concept characterization in its entirety.

properties.name: objectStatus
properties.description: The current state of formality and acceptance for a UCO object.

properties.name: specVersion
properties.description: The version of UCO ontology or subontology specification used to characterize a concept.

properties.name: tag
properties.description: A generic tag/label.

URLVisitFacet.description: A URL visit facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the properties of a visit of a URL within a particular browser.

properties.name: browserInformation
properties.description: Specifies information about the particular Web Browser.

properties.name: fromURLVisit
properties.description: Specifies the URL visit origination point (i.e., URL) of the URL captured in the URL history entry, if applicable.

properties.name: url
properties.description: Specifies a URL associated with a particular observable object or facet.

properties.name: urlTransitionType
properties.description: Specifies how a browser navigated to a particular URL on a particular visit.

properties.name: visitDuration
properties.description: Specifies the duration of a specific visit of a URL within a particular browser.

properties.name: visitTime
properties.description: Specifies the date/time of a specific visit of a URL within a particular browser.

UcoInherentCharacterizationThing.description: A UCO inherent characterization thing is a grouping of characteristics unique to a particular inherent aspect of a UCO domain object.

UcoObject.description: A UCO object is a representation of a fundamental concept either directly inherent to the cyber domain or indirectly related to the cyber domain and necessary for contextually characterizing cyber domain concepts and relationships. Within the Unified Cyber Ontology (UCO) structure this is the base class acting as a consistent, unifying and interoperable foundation for all explicit and inter-relatable content objects.

UserSession.description: A user session is a temporary and interactive information interchange between two or more communicating devices within the managed scope of a single user. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Session_(computer_science)]

properties.name: effectiveGroup
properties.description: Specifies the name of the effective group used in the user session.

properties.name: effectiveGroupID
properties.description: Specifies the effective group ID of the group used in the user session.

properties.name: effectiveUser
properties.description: Specifies the effective user details used in the user session.

properties.name: loginTime
properties.description: Specifies the date/time of the login for the user session.

properties.name: logoutTime
properties.description: Specifies the date/time of the logout for the user session.

ValuesEnumeratedEffectFacet.description: A values enumerated effect facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to the effects of actions upon observable objects where a value of the observable object is enumerated. An example of this would be the values of a registry key.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values that were enumerated as a result of the action on the object.

Victim.description: A victim is a role played by a person or organization that is/was the target of some malicious action.

properties.name: actionCount
properties.description: The number of times that the action was performed.

properties.name: actionStatus
properties.description: The current state of the action.

properties.name: endTime
properties.description: The time at which performance of the action ended.

properties.name: environment
properties.description: The environment wherein an action occurs.

properties.name: error
properties.description: A characterization of the differences between the expected and the actual performance of the action.

properties.name: instrument
properties.description: The things used to perform an action.

properties.name: location
properties.description: The locations where an action occurs.

properties.name: object
properties.description: The things that the action is performed on/against.

properties.name: participant
properties.description: The supporting (non-primary) performers of an action.

properties.name: performer
properties.description: The primary performer of an action.

properties.name: result
properties.description: The things resulting from performing an action.

properties.name: startTime
properties.description: The time at which performance of the action began.

properties.name: subaction
properties.description: References to other actions that make up part of a larger more complex action.

properties.name: phase
properties.description: The ordered set of actions or sub action-lifecycles that represent the action lifecycle.

VictimTargeting.description: A victim targeting is a grouping of characteristics unique to people or organizations that are the target of some malicious activity.

WhoIsFacet.description: A whois facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a response record conformant to the WHOIS protocol standard (RFC 3912). [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WHOIS]

properties.name: creationDate
properties.description: Specifies the date in which the registered domain was created.

properties.name: dnssec
properties.description: Specifies the DNSSEC property associated with a Whois entry. Acceptable values are: 'Signed' or 'Unsigned'.

properties.name: domainID
properties.description: Specifies the domain id for the domain associated with a Whois entry.

properties.name: domainName
properties.description: Specifies the corresponding domain name for a whois entry.

properties.name: expirationDate
properties.description: Specifies the date in which the registered domain will expire.

properties.name: ipAddress
properties.description: Specifies the corresponding ip address for a whois entry. Usually corresponds to a name server lookup.

properties.name: lookupDate
properties.description: Specifies the date and time that the Whois record was queried.

properties.name: nameServer
properties.description: Specifies a list of name server entries for a Whois entry.

properties.name: regionalInternetRegistry
properties.description: specifies the name of the Regional Internet Registry (RIR) which allocated the IP address contained in a WHOIS entry.

properties.name: registrantContactInfo
properties.description: Specifies contact info for the registrant of a domain within a WHOIS entity.

properties.name: registrantIDs
properties.description: Specifies the registrant IDs associated with a domain lookup.

properties.name: registrarInfo
properties.description: Specifies registrar info that would be returned from a registrar lookup.

properties.name: remarks
properties.description: Specifies any remarks associated with this Whois entry.

properties.name: serverName
properties.description: Specifies the corresponding server name for a whois entry. This usually corresponds to a name server lookup.

properties.name: sponsoringRegistrar
properties.description: Specifies the name of the sponsoring registrar for a domain.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Specifies a list of statuses for a given Whois entry.

properties.name: updatedDate
properties.description: Specifies the date in which the registered domain information was last updated.

WhoisContactFacet.description: A Whois contact type is a grouping of characteristics unique to contact-related information present in a response record conformant to the WHOIS protocol standard (RFC 3912). [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WHOIS]

properties.name: contactAddress
properties.description: Contact address specifies information characterizing a geolocation address of a contact entity.

properties.name: contactAffiliation
properties.description: Contact affiliation specifies information characterizing details of an organizational affiliation for a single contact entity.

properties.name: contactEmail
properties.description: Contact email specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by email.

properties.name: contactGroup
properties.description: Contact group specifies the name/tag of a particular named/tagged grouping of contacts.

properties.name: contactID
properties.description: Specifies an ID for the contact.

properties.name: contactMessaging
properties.description: Contact messaging specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by digital messaging.

properties.name: contactNote
properties.description: Contact note specifies a comment/note associated with a given contact.

properties.name: contactPhone
properties.description: Contact phone specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by telephone.

properties.name: contactProfile
properties.description: Contact profile specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by online service.

properties.name: contactSIP
properties.description: Contact SIP specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by Session Initiation Protocol (SIP).

properties.name: contactURL
properties.description: Contact URL specifies information characterizing details for contacting a contact entity by Uniform Resource Locator (URL).

properties.name: displayName
properties.description: Display name specifies the name to display for some entity within a user interface.

properties.name: firstName
properties.description: The first name of a person.

properties.name: lastName
properties.description: The last name of a person.

properties.name: lastTimeContacted
properties.description: Last time contacted specifies the date and time that a particular contact was last contacted.

properties.name: middleName
properties.description: The middle name of a person.

properties.name: namePhonetic
properties.description: Name phonetic specifies the phonetic pronunciation of the name of a person.

properties.name: namePrefix
properties.description: Name prefix specifies an honorific prefix (coming ordinally before first/given name) for the name of a person.

properties.name: nameSuffix
properties.description: Name suffix specifies an suffix (coming ordinally after last/family name) for the name of a person.

properties.name: nickname
properties.description: Nickname specifies an alternate, unofficial and typically informal name for a person independent of their official name.

properties.name: numberTimesContacted
properties.description: Number times contacted specifies the number of times a particular contact has been contacted.

properties.name: sourceApplication
properties.description: Source application specifies the software application that a particular contact or contact list is associated with.

properties.name: whoisContactType
properties.description: Specifies what type of WHOIS contact this is.

WhoisRegistrarInfoType.description: A Whois registrar info type is a grouping of characteristics unique to registrar-related information present in a response record conformant to the WHOIS protocol standard (RFC 3912). [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WHOIS]

properties.name: contactPhoneNumber
properties.description: Contact phone number specifies a telephone service account number for contacting a contact entity by telephone.

properties.name: emailAddress
properties.description: An email address.

properties.name: geolocationAddress
properties.description: An administrative address for a particular geolocation.

properties.name: referralURL
properties.description: Specifies the corresponding referral URL for a registrar.

properties.name: registrarGUID
properties.description: Specifies the Registrar GUID field of a Whois entry.

properties.name: registrarID
properties.description: Specifies the Registrar ID field of a Whois entry.

properties.name: registrarName
properties.description: The name of the registrar organization.

properties.name: whoisServer
properties.description: Specifies the corresponding whois server for a registrar.

WifiAddress.description: A Wi-Fi address is a media access control (MAC) standards-conformant identifier assigned to a device network interface to enable routing and management of IEEE 802.11 standards-conformant communications to and from that device.

Wiki.description: A wiki is an online hypertext publication collaboratively edited and managed by its own audience directly using a web browser. A typical wiki contains multiple pages/articles for the subjects or scope of the project and could be either open to the public or limited to use within an organization for maintaining its internal knowledge base. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wiki]

properties.name: msProductID
properties.description: The Microsoft Product ID. See also: http://support.microsoft.com/gp/pidwin.

properties.name: msProductName
properties.description: The Microsoft ProductName of the current installation of Windows. This is typically found in HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion!ProductName.

properties.name: netBIOSName
properties.description: Specifies the NetBIOS (Network Basic Input/Output System) name of the Windows system. This is not the same as the host name.

properties.name: osLastUpgradeDate
properties.description: Specifies the date on which the operating system (OS) was last upgraded.

properties.name: registeredOrganization
properties.description: The organization that this copy of Windows is registered to.

properties.name: registeredOwner
properties.description: The person or organization that is the registered owner of this copy of Windows.

properties.name: windowsDirectory
properties.description: The Windows_Directory field specifies the fully-qualified path to the Windows install directory.

properties.name: windowsSystemDirectory
properties.description: The Windows_System_Directory field specifies the fully-qualified path to the Windows system directory.

properties.name: windowsTempDirectory
properties.description: The Windows_Temp_Directory field specifies the fully-qualified path to the Windows temporary files directory.

WindowsCriticalSection.description: A Windows critical section is a Windows object that provides synchronization similar to that provided by a mutex object, except that a critical section can be used only by the threads of a single process. Critical section objects cannot be shared across processes. Event, mutex, and semaphore objects can also be used in a single-process application, but critical section objects provide a slightly faster, more efficient mechanism for mutual-exclusion synchronization (a processor-specific test and set instruction). Like a mutex object, a critical section object can be owned by only one thread at a time, which makes it useful for protecting a shared resource from simultaneous access. Unlike a mutex object, there is no way to tell whether a critical section has been abandoned. [based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/sync/critical-section-objects]

WindowsFilemapping.description: A Windows file mapping is the association of a file's contents with a portion of the virtual address space of a process within a Windows operating system. The system creates a file mapping object (also known as a section object) to maintain this association. A file view is the portion of virtual address space that a process uses to access the file's contents. File mapping allows the process to use both random input and output (I/O) and sequential I/O. It also allows the process to work efficiently with a large data file, such as a database, without having to map the whole file into memory. Multiple processes can also use memory-mapped files to share data. Processes read from and write to the file view using pointers, just as they would with dynamically allocated memory. The use of file mapping improves efficiency because the file resides on disk, but the file view resides in memory.[based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/memory/file-mapping]

WindowsHook.description: A Windows hook is a mechanism by which an application can intercept events, such as messages, mouse actions, and keystrokes within the Windows operating system. A function that intercepts a particular type of event is known as a hook procedure. A hook procedure can act on each event it receives, and then modify or discard the event. [based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/winmsg/about-hooks]

WindowsMailslot.description: A Windows mailslot is is a pseudofile that resides in memory, and may be accessed using standard file functions. The data in a mailslot message can be in any form, but cannot be larger than 424 bytes when sent between computers. Unlike disk files, mailslots are temporary. When all handles to a mailslot are closed, the mailslot and all the data it contains are deleted. [based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/ipc/about-mailslots]

WindowsNetworkShare.description: A Windows network share is a Windows computer resource made available from one host to other hosts on a computer network. It is a device or piece of information on a computer that can be remotely accessed from another computer transparently as if it were a resource in the local machine. Network sharing is made possible by inter-process communication over the network. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shared_resource]

properties.name: characteristics
properties.description: Specifies the flags that indicate the fileâ€™s characteristics.

properties.name: fileHeaderHashes
properties.description: Specifies any hashes that were computed for the file header.

properties.name: impHash
properties.description: Specifies the special import hash, or â€˜imphashâ€™, calculated for the PE Binary based on its imported libraries and functions.

properties.name: machine
properties.description: Specifies the type of target machine.

properties.name: numberOfSections
properties.description: Specifies the number of sections in the PE binary, as a non-negative integer.

properties.name: numberOfSymbols
properties.description: Specifies the number of entries in the symbol table of the PE binary, as a non-negative integer.

properties.name: optionalHeader
properties.description: Specifies the PE optional header of the PE binary.

properties.name: peType
properties.description: Specifies the type of the PE binary.

properties.name: pointerToSymbolTable
properties.description: Specifies the file offset of the COFF symbol table.

properties.name: sections
properties.description: Specifies metadata about the sections in the PE file.

properties.name: sizeOfOptionalHeader
properties.description: Specifies the size of the optional header of the PE binary.

properties.name: timeDateStamp
properties.description: Specifies the time when the PE binary was created.

WindowsPEFileHeader.description: A Windows PE file header is a grouping of characteristics unique to the 'header' of a Windows PE (Portable Executable) file, consisting of a collection of metadata about the overall nature and structure of the file.

properties.name: timeDateStamp
properties.description: Specifies the time when the PE binary was created.

WindowsPEOptionalHeader.description: A Windows PE optional header is a grouping of characteristics unique to the 'optional header' of a Windows PE (Portable Executable) file, consisting of a collection of metadata about the executable code structure of the file.

properties.name: addressOfEntryPoint
properties.description: Specifies the address of the entry point relative to the image base when the executable is loaded into memory.

properties.name: baseOfCode
properties.description: Specifies the address that is relative to the image base of the beginning-of-code section when it is loaded into memory.

properties.name: checksum
properties.description: Specifies the checksum of the PE binary.

properties.name: dllCharacteristics
properties.description: Specifies the flags that characterize the PE binary.

properties.name: fileAlignment
properties.description: Specifies the factor (in bytes) that is used to align the raw data of sections in the image file.

properties.name: imageBase
properties.description: Specifies the address that is relative to the image base of the beginning-of-data section when it is loaded into memory.

properties.name: loaderFlags
properties.description: Specifies the reserved loader flags

properties.name: magic
properties.description: Specifies the value that indicates the type of the PE binary.

properties.name: majorImageVersion
properties.description: Specifies the major version number of the image.

properties.name: majorLinkerVersion
properties.description: Specifies the linker major version number.

properties.name: majorOSVersion
properties.description: Specifies the major version number of the required operating system.

properties.name: majorSubsystemVersion
properties.description: Specifies the major version number of the subsystem.

properties.name: minorImageVersion
properties.description: Specifies the minor version number of the image.

properties.name: minorLinkerVersion
properties.description: Specifies the linker minor version number.

properties.name: minorOSVersion
properties.description: Specifies the minor version number of the required operating system.

properties.name: minorSubsystemVersion
properties.description: Specifies the minor version number of the subsystem.

properties.name: numberOfRVAAndSizes
properties.description: Specifies the number of data-directory entries in the remainder of the optional header.

properties.name: sectionAlignment
properties.description: Specifies the alignment (in bytes) of PE sections when they are loaded into memory.

properties.name: sizeOfCode
properties.description: Specifies the size of the code (text) section. If there are multiple such sections, this refers to the sum of the sizes of each section.

properties.name: sizeOfHeaders
properties.description: Specifies the combined size of the MS-DOS, PE header, and section headers, rounded up a multiple of the value specified in the file_alignment header.

properties.name: sizeOfHeapCommit
properties.description: Specifies the size of the local heap space to commit.

properties.name: sizeOfHeapReserve
properties.description: Specifies the size of the local heap space to reserve.

properties.name: sizeOfImage
properties.description: Specifies the size, in bytes, of the image, including all headers, as the image is loaded in memory.

properties.name: sizeOfInitializedData
properties.description: Specifies the size of the initialized data section. If there are multiple such sections, this refers to the sum of the sizes of each section.

properties.name: sizeOfStackCommit
properties.description: Specifies the size of the stack to commit.

properties.name: sizeOfStackReserve
properties.description: Specifies the size of the stack to reserve.

properties.name: sizeOfUninitializedData
properties.description: Specifies the size of the uninitialized data section. If there are multiple such sections, this refers to the sum of the sizes of each section.

properties.name: subsystem
properties.description: Specifies the subsystem (e.g., GUI, device driver, etc.) that is required to run this image.

properties.name: win32VersionValue
properties.description: Specifies the reserved win32 version value.

WindowsPESection.description: A Windows PE section is a grouping of characteristics unique to a specific default or custom-defined region of a Windows PE (Portable Executable) file, consisting of an individual portion of the actual executable content of the file delineated according to unique purpose and memory protection requirements.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: entropy
properties.description: Shannon entropy (a measure of randomness) of the data.

properties.name: hashes
properties.description: Specifies any hashes computed over the section.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Specifies the size of the section, in bytes.

WindowsPrefetch.description: The Windows prefetch contains entries in a Windows prefetch file (used to speed up application startup starting with Windows XP).

properties.name: createdBy
properties.description: The identity that created a characterization of a concept.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: externalReference
properties.description: Specifies a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: hasFacet
properties.description: Further sets of properties characterizing a concept based on the particular context of the class and of the particular instance of the concept being characterized.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: objectCreatedTime
properties.description: The time at which a characterization of a concept is created. This time pertains to the time of creating the record object, and is not an intrinsic characteristic of the concept.

properties.name: objectMarking
properties.description: Marking definitions to be applied to a particular concept characterization in its entirety.

properties.name: objectStatus
properties.description: The current state of formality and acceptance for a UCO object.

properties.name: specVersion
properties.description: The version of UCO ontology or subontology specification used to characterize a concept.

properties.name: tag
properties.description: A generic tag/label.

WindowsPrefetchFacet.description: A Windows prefetch facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to entries in the Windows prefetch file (used to speed up application startup starting with Windows XP).

properties.name: accessedDirectory
properties.description: Directories accessed by the prefetch application during startup.

properties.name: accessedFile
properties.description: Files (e.g., DLLs and other support files) used by the application during startup.

properties.name: applicationFileName
properties.description: Name of the executable of the prefetch file.

properties.name: firstRun
properties.description: Timestamp of when the prefetch application was first run.

properties.name: lastRun
properties.description: Timestamp of when the prefetch application was last run.

properties.name: prefetchHash
properties.description: An eight character hash of the location from which the application was run.

properties.name: timesExecuted
properties.description: The number of times the prefetch application has executed.

properties.name: volume
properties.description: The volume from which the prefetch application was run. If the applicatin was run from multiple volumes, there will be a separate prefetch file for each.

WindowsSystemRestore.description: A Windows system restore is a capture of a Windows computer's state (including system files, installed applications, Windows Registry, and system settings) at a particular point in time such that the computer can be reverted to that state in the event of system malfunctions or other problems. [based on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/System_Restore]

WindowsTaskFacet.description: A Windows Task facet is a grouping of characteristics unique to a Windows Task (a process that is scheduled to execute on a Windows operating system by the Windows Task Scheduler). [based on http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381311(v=vs.85).aspx]

properties.name: account
properties.description: Specifies the account referenced in an event log entry or used to run the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381228(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: accountLogonType
properties.description: Specifies the security logon method required to run the tasks associated with the account. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa383013(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: accountRunLevel
properties.description: Specifies the permission level of the account that the task will be run at.

properties.name: actionList
properties.description: Specifies a list of actions to be performed by the scheduled task.

properties.name: application
properties.description: The application associated with this object.

properties.name: exitCode
properties.description: Specifies the last exit code of the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381245(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: flags
properties.description: Specifies any flags that modify the behavior of the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381248(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: imageName
properties.description: Specifies the image name for the task.

properties.name: maxRunTime
properties.description: Specifies the maximum run time of the scheduled task before terminating, in milliseconds. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381874(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: mostRecentRunTime
properties.description: Specifies the most recent run date/time of this scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381254(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: nextRunTime
properties.description: Specifies the next run date/time of the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381257(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: observableCreatedTime
properties.description: The date and time at which the observable object being characterized was created. This time pertains to an intrinsic characteristic of the observable object, and would be consistent across independent characterizations or observations of the observable object.

properties.name: parameters
properties.description: Specifies the command line parameters used to launch the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381875(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: priority
properties.description: The priority of the email.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Specifies a list of statuses for a given Whois entry.

properties.name: taskComment
properties.description: Specifies a comment for the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381232(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: taskCreator
properties.description: Specifies the name of the creator of the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381235(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: triggerList
properties.description: Specifies a set of triggers used by the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa383264(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: workItemData
properties.description: Specifies application defined data associated with the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381271(v=vs.85).aspx.

properties.name: workingDirectory
properties.description: Specifies the working directory for the scheduled task. See also: http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa381878(v=vs.85).aspx.

WindowsWaitableTime.description: A Windows waitable timer is a synchronization object within the Windows operating system whose state is set to signaled when a specified due time arrives. There are two types of waitable timers that can be created: manual-reset and synchronization. A timer of either type can also be a periodic timer. [based on https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/sync/waitable-timer-objects]

properties.name: externalReference
properties.description: Specifies a reference to a resource outside of the UCO.

properties.name: hasFacet
properties.description: Further sets of properties characterizing a concept based on the particular context of the class and of the particular instance of the concept being characterized.

properties.name: modifiedTime
properties.description: Specifies the time that this particular version of the object was modified. The object creator can use the time it deems most appropriate as the time this version of the object was modified. The value of the modified property for a given object version MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. Object creators MUST update the modified property when creating a new version of an object. The modified timestamp MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds).

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of a particular concept characterization.

properties.name: objectCreatedTime
properties.description: The time at which a characterization of a concept is created. This time pertains to the time of creating the record object, and is not an intrinsic characteristic of the concept.
